_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Ability to assess Data in Transit encryption policies.
reference_document_element_description: Knowledge of data protection law, including GDPR, CCPA/CPRA, and sector-specific privacy regulations, with emphasis on the legal bases for processing, data subject rights, cross-border transfer mechanisms, and enforcement precedent.
comments: DCWF ksa intersects with WIDAI SP domain. SP-K-007 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: SP-K-014 (SP, cosine: 0.499), SP-K-002 (SP, cosine: 0.483).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 3.9/10 (raw 0.391). Shared vocabulary: [data]. DCWF-only terms [ability, assess, encryption, policies, transit] vs WIDAI-only terms [bases, border, ccpa, cpra, cross, emphasis] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.860 (P=0.885, R=0.837). P exceeds R by 0.048 — the DCWF element's 8 words are well-captured by the WIDAI KSA's 29 words, but the KSA contains additional specificity not reflected in the DCWF element.

nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (98.5%). "Ability to assess Data in Transit encryption policies." and "Knowledge of data protection law, including GDPR, CCPA/CPRA, and sector-specific" are related but neither implies nor contradicts the other. Standard result for cross-framework mapping where two independent frameworks describe overlapping competencies with independent vocabulary.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.513 > STS 0.391 (gap +0.122). Independent encoding finds more similarity than cross-attention. Shared surface terms [data] inflate the bi-encoder, but cross-attention detects semantic divergence beneath the vocabulary overlap.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 2.7%. Shared tokens: [data]. DCWF-only: [ability, assess, encryption, policies, transit]. WIDAI-only: [bases, border, ccpa, cpra, cross, emphasis].

signals.detail: Bi-encoder/STS gap 0.122 suggests some vocabulary-driven inflation in candidate selection.
